author: Morax
message:
feat: add support for WeChat build 269079 (4.1.11 hotfix)

269079 is a 4.1.11 hotfix that rebased the whole arm64 slice, so every
site shifted vs 269077 — all patch points were re-derived and verified
against the installed wechat.dylib:

- revoke / revoke-tip: parseRevokeXML relocated to 0x48a7c4c (unique
  geometry match); cbz at entry+0x270, str x0,[x19,#0x168] at entry+0xA04.
- runtime-tip: inline-hook entry rewrite with SLOT 0x93b7f00 in the
  __DATA tail zero-fill; adrp/ldr/br round-trips through decodeEntryStubSlot.
- update: 8 XAppUpdateManager sites re-located by selector->IMP; byte
  patterns match 269077 semantics (accessor fields 0x18/0x19).

Field offsets 0x168/0x170 were re-decoded from this binary's own
instructions. Registered in patches.json, supportedBuildVersions, and
inlineRevokeHookConfigs; added a PatchConfig coverage test and updated
the injector error-string test.

install --dry-run confirms all 12 sites match; swift build + 90 tests green.
Runtime behavior (hook firing, update blocking) still needs a live 269079
test; Sparkle/SPUUpdater channel remains uncovered.

author: Morax
message:
docs: rewrite README for readability, split detail into MAINTAINING.md

- Restructure README as a user-facing guide: feature overview, a version
  capability matrix (build x silent/tip/custom-tip/block-update/multi),
  command and flag tables, and a single consolidated post-install
  verification note instead of per-build caveats.
- Move byte-level patch addresses, the two runtime-hook mechanisms
  (dispatch stub vs inline), update-block derivation, per-build byte
  records, and the "add a new build" workflow into a new MAINTAINING.md.
- Correct the {content} description to match the committed runtime: no
  installed hook populates the content cache (installRevokeTipHook only
  hooks parseRevokeXML), so {content} renders empty at runtime on every
  build; only the render/preview path substitutes it.

author: Morax
message:
fix(ci): detached checkout so fork PRs named like default branch don't collide

The @claude (issue_comment) workflow checked out the default branch as a
local branch (refs/heads/main). claude-code-action then fetches a fork PR
head into a branch named after the fork's head ref; for a fork PR opened
from the fork's own "main", that is `git fetch origin pull/N/head:main`,
which git refuses because refs/heads/main is checked out.

Use `ref: ${{ github.sha }}` to check out detached so no local default
branch ref exists for the action's fetch to collide with.

Upstream bug: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code-action/issues/1423

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Morax
message:
feat: add {content} recall-tip placeholder foundation and WeChat 268851 support

Custom recall tip (--runtime-tip) can now reference {content}, the recalled
message's text; media shows a localized type placeholder like [图片]/[语音].
The render/idempotency pipeline (keeps {content} out of dangling separators and
survives re-parse), an in-memory newmsgid->content cache, the preview mapper,
CLI preview plumbing, README docs and unit tests are all in place. The receive-
path hook that fills the cache is still pending, so on a cold cache {content}
renders empty (leading separator stripped) with no behavior change.

The debug probe now logs via os_log %{public} (NSLog redacted every dynamic
field to <private> on macOS 26/27) and dumps the revoke message struct to help
locate the recalled-content source.

Also add WeChat 4.1.10 build 268851: a byte-identical hotfix of 268850 (all 13
patch sites and the inline-hook SLOT at 0x952bf00 verified identical against the
live dylib), wired into patches.json, the runtime inline-hook config and
RuntimeTipInstaller.supportedBuildVersions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Morax
message:
feat: Add inline hook support for WeChat builds 268849 and 268850

- Implemented inline hooking mechanism for WeChat builds lacking a dispatch stub in `parseRevokeXML`.
- Introduced `InlineRevokeHookConfig` structure to manage inline hook configurations.
- Added functions for encoding and decoding entry stubs, enabling dynamic hooking.
- Enhanced XML processing to restore `newmsgid` for self-recalls, ensuring native deletion behavior.
- Updated patch configurations in `patches.json` to include new builds.
- Expanded unit tests to cover new functionality, including inline hook engine tests and self-recall handling.
- Modified existing tests to reflect support for new build versions.
